name: The Havana National Bank
description_short: Havana National Bank is a locally owned community bank established in 1875, serving west-central Illinois with multiple branch locations and FDIC insurance.
description_long:
Havana National Bank opened for business on May 17, 1875, and has continuously served west-central Illinois for nearly 150 years. The bank is locally owned and positioned as a hometown community bank with a strong agricultural focus. It maintains its headquarters in Havana, Illinois, along the Illinois River, with branch locations in Lewistown, San Jose, Green Valley, Manito, and Virginia.

The bank offers standard banking services including online banking with login capabilities, checking and savings accounts, Certificate of Deposit (CD) products, and deposit accounts. They provide financial calculators and an Agricultural Pricing Index to serve their farming community. The bank is FDIC-insured, meaning deposits are backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government up to applicable limits. They recently announced enhancements to their online banking platform and mobile app launching February 13, 2025.

Havana National Bank distinguishes itself through its deep community roots and agricultural specialization. The bank emphasizes its 150-year history of serving the same communities and positions itself as a relationship-focused institution rather than a large national chain. Their specific focus on agricultural customers, evidenced by their Agricultural Pricing Index offering, sets them apart from generic regional banks.

As a traditional community bank, Havana National Bank is best suited for customers seeking local banking relationships and those with agricultural or rural financing needs. The website provides standard banking information but offers limited detail on specific product rates, loan terms, or comprehensive service offerings beyond basic deposit products. Their commitment to community engagement is evident through educational content on fraud prevention and customer service. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Mobile banking app (enhanced version launching February 2025)
Blog with financial education and how-to guides
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
150-year history of continuous operation serving the same communities
Agricultural Pricing Index and specialized agricultural banking focus
Multiple branch locations across west-central Illinois (5 branches listed)
Online banking with login and enrollment capabilities
Educational resources including HNB Blog with fraud prevention guides
Locally owned and operated community bank with personal relationship focus
cons: Website lacks specific information on CD rates, deposit rates, and loan terms
Limited geographic reach (only west-central Illinois locations)
No clear information about business banking, commercial loans, or credit products
Online banking platform requires login credentials; limited details on digital features before enrollment
No mention of mobile app features, payment options, or digital wallet integration
best_for: Agricultural and farming customers seeking specialized lending and pricing
West-central Illinois residents seeking local banking relationships
Community-oriented customers preferring established, locally-owned institutions
Customers seeking FDIC-insured deposit accounts with personal service
diagnosis: Havana National Bank is best for rural and agricultural customers in west-central Illinois seeking community-focused banking with FDIC insurance protection. The main caveat is that the website provides minimal detail on specific rates, loan products, or comprehensive service offerings, requiring direct contact to learn about full product availability and terms.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
